title: Section 62A Planning Application: S62A/2025/0107  Former Friends School Field, Mount Pleasant Road, Saffron Walden, CB11 3EB
body: "All documents, excluding the hearing notice (if one is required) and decision once made, relating to this case published after 24 June 2025 will now be available on the additional documents page  Type of application: Full Planning Application  Applicant name  Chase New Homes  Site address:  Former Friends School Field, Mount Pleasant Road, Saffron Walden, CB11 3EB  Description of proposed development  Erection of 75no. dwellings with associated infrastructure and landscaping. Provision of playing fields and associated clubhouse.  Case Officer  Section 62A Applications Team  Procedure  Hearing  Key dates      Date     Date application valid 27 May 2025   Date by when representations must be made 4 July 2025   Hearing date and venue 10.00am on Friday 8 August 2025 at Council Chamber, Uttlesford District Council, London Road, Saffron Walden, CB11 4E  Hearing Notification  (PDF, 81.2 KB, 1 page)    Target decision 1 September 2025   Decision date 1 September 2025     Hearing Documents   Issues Report and Agenda  (PDF, 169 KB, 5 pages)  Friends Fields Signed UU dated 31.7.25  (PDF, 14.6 MB, 109 pages)  UU August 25  (PDF, 191 KB, 3 pages)  Draft_Planning_Conditions_Former Friends School_01Aug2025(3)  (PDF, 105 KB, 13 pages)  CIL_Compliance_Statement_Former Friends School_01Aug2025  (PDF, 156 KB, 6 pages)  UDC re proposed pedestrian access condition  (PDF, 125 KB, 1 page)   Decision   Decision Notice and Statement of Reasons  (PDF, 369 KB, 34 pages)   Make a representation  The application can be inspected under the application plans and documents heading below.  You will be able to comment on this application until 4 July 2025   Please base your comments on planning matters.  Please avoid the use of inflammatory, discriminatory or abusive comments  All representations made in connection with the development proposal will be made publicly available on the website shortly after the end of the representation period.  The name of the individual author or organisation will be published however addresses, signatures and contact details will not be published.  We advise you not to include any other personal information about yourself or others.   Our Customer Privacy Notice sets out how we handle personal data in accordance with the law.  Send your comments via email to section62a@planninginspectorate.gov.uk  or write to  Section 62A Applications Team,Planning Inspectorate,3rd Floor, Temple Quay House,2 The Square, Temple Quay,Bristol,BS1 6PN   Sending comments by email will enable us to deal with them more quickly.  Please include the application reference number S62A/2025/0107 and site address.  This application will be determined by an Inspector appointed by the Secretary of State.  Shortly after the close of the representation period the Planning Inspectorate will consider whether a hearing is necessary.   In the event that a hearing is scheduled, and you would wish to speak at the hearing, you must make this request at the time you submit your written representations.  Where the application is to be considered at a hearing, notice of the date, time and venue for the hearing along with the name of the person holding the hearing, will be published on this website at least two weeks before the date of the hearing.    All documents, excluding the hearing notice (if one is required) and decision once made, relating...
